systemStatus.headline: Tue Jul 21, 7 AM live run. Monday Jul 20 Toast landed: net $3,799.70 / 151 guests / 102 orders, up +38.2% vs Mon Jul 13 -- a clearly stronger Monday, though labor at 34.9% of net is the number to eyeball (normal for a low-volume Monday; last Monday was 35.8%). Sling Tue Jul 21: 9 employees / 9 shifts / 38.23 hrs, 0 uncovered, 2 malformed zero-length entries (Isael, Casey Smith). Google Ads Jul 20: $49.17 / 6,911 impr / 136 clicks / 0 conv. 'World Cup HQ 2026' ($20/day) STILL paused two days after the Final -- archive it. Yelp audience size flipped 'Limited' -> 'Ideal'.
systemStatus.detail:
Tue Jul 21 07:00 AM PT run. Live pass -- Google Ads and Yelp dashboards were both scraped this run.

TOAST (Mon Jul 20, group-level): net $3,799.70 / gross $3,873.70 / 151 guests / 102 orders / avg guest $25.16 / avg order $37.25 / labor 34.9% / discounts 1.9% / refunds $0 / 4 void items / 83 card payments. vs SDLW (Mon Jul 13): net +38.2%, gross +37.4%, guests +25.8%, orders +12.1%. A low-dollar Monday but up sharply on the prior Monday. Labor 34.9% of net looks high, yet it is a fixed-cost-on-low-volume artifact -- Mon Jul 13 ran 35.8% -- so week-over-week labor efficiency actually improved. One order left open at close; Julianna Ornelas and Harmony Willson auto-clocked out. The LOCATION-level email (labor $, void $, dayparts, rooms) had not landed at run time.

SLING (Tue Jul 21): 9 staff / 9 shifts / 38.23 hrs / 0 uncovered / 0 OT / 0 off. Two malformed zero-length entries persist -- Isael Salvador Narciso (Cook) 4:41A-4:41A and Casey Smith (GM) 6:07A-6:07A -- both read as clock-in placeholders, not real shifts. Real coverage: Sarah Palmer opens (6:30A-12:30P), Tony Rincon on bar midday, a single server (Christine Vargas) 11:30A-3:30P, then the evening turns to Andrew Turner (bar), Augustine Arroyo + Nathan Smith (servers) and Cal Carter (busser) closing 9:30-10:30P. The single-server midday block is the thin spot.

GOOGLE ADS (Jul 20, live): $49.17 / 6,911 impr / 136 clicks / CTR 1.97% / CPC $0.36 / 0 conversions (calculating). Only 2 of 4 campaigns served. PMax-2 carried 97% of impressions (6,707) and 126 clicks while flagged both 'Limited by budget' and all-asset-groups-policy-limited. BHC Coffee Search: 204 impr, 10 clicks, opt 44.5%. 'World Cup HQ 2026' ($20/day) is STILL PAUSED with 0 impressions two days past the Final -- highest-leverage stale item, archive it. 'Oldest SB Brewery on Tap' ($10.80/day) also paused, policy-limited. Account budget $58.37/day; 2 drafts unpublished; 'Review your campaign targets' banner (Aug 17 2026 deadline) still active. Conversions have read 0 for many runs -- a tracking question.

YELP (last 30d, live): 30.2k ad impressions (73% of 41.6k), 391 ad clicks, 532 page visits from ads (57%), 146 leads from ads (59% of 248). 12-mo 1,126 ad leads vs 741 organic (60/40), forecast 88. NOTABLE: 'Estimated audience size' now reads 'Ideal' (was 'Limited' on prior runs) -- Yelp says the targeting is now typical for similar local businesses. Budget $13/day, 5-mile radius. Billing (spend/CPC/CPL) NOT re-read -- the billing page is behind a password wall -- so Jul 1-19 $264.51 / CPC $0.99 / CPL $2.88 are carried from Mon Jul 20. Bonus budget remaining still not surfaced (6th run). Yelp Inbox: 15 unread.

TRIPADVISOR: a teaser ad-engagement email from analytics@hm2.tripadvisor.com arrived Mon Jul 20 12:30 PM PT with no parseable figures (numbers only appear after clicking through).

NOT REFRESHED THIS RUN (out of scope -- job covers Toast/Sling/Google/Yelp/TripAdvisor): reviews, reservations, Yelp inquiries, business profile, payroll. Nothing was posted or sent from this run.

toast.source: GROUP-level Daily Performance Summary (The Brewhouse Santa Barbara Group, Mon Jul 20), email received Tue Jul 21 6:25 AM PT. LOCATION-level email (labor $, void $, dayparts, rooms, checks) had not landed at the 7 AM run. SDLW computed locally against the Mon Jul 13 GROUP email.
toast.note: MON JUL 20 ACTUALS (group-level). Net $3,799.70 / Gross $3,873.70 / 151 guests / 102 orders / avg guest $25.16 / avg order $37.25. Labor 34.9% of net (~$1,326) -- high as a percentage, but this is a low-volume Monday and last Monday (Jul 13) ran 35.8%, so labor is actually slightly better week-over-week. Discounts 1.9% (~$74), refunds $0, deferred $0, 4 void items, 83 card payments. ONE order left open at close; Julianna Ornelas and Harmony Willson auto-clocked out. vs SDLW (Mon Jul 13): net +$1,049.65 (+38.2%), gross +37.4%, guests +31 (+25.8%), orders +11 (+12.1%). A clearly stronger Monday than the week prior.

sling.note: TODAY (Tue Jul 21), email delivered 7:01 AM PT. 9 employees / 9 shifts / 38.23 hrs / 0 uncovered / 0 OT / 0 off. A lighter Tuesday build. TWO malformed zero-length entries remain: Isael Salvador Narciso (Cook) 4:41A-4:41A and Casey Smith (General Manager) 6:07A-6:07A -- both look like clock-in placeholders rather than real shifts. Coverage: Sarah Palmer opens on coffee/bar (6:30A-12:30P), Tony Rincon on bar midday, one server (Christine Vargas) 11:30A-3:30P, then the evening turns over to Andrew Turner (bar), Augustine Arroyo and Nathan Smith (servers) and Cal Carter (busser) closing 9:30-10:30P. Thin single-server midday block is the spot to watch.

reviews.lastUpdated: 2026-07-21 -- Tue Jul 21 4 AM run. Gmail delta on businessprofile-noreply@google.com + no-reply@spothopperapp.com. BOTH listings checked.
reviews.note: BOTH GOOGLE LISTINGS CHECKED. Direct load of the Unreplied tabs again returned Google's search-embedded merchant view, so review lists were sourced from Gmail notifications. THE BREWHOUSE: 2 unreplied -- Ronald Johnson (3 stars, Jul 19, World Cup Final: no sightlines + flat beer) and Thuy Anh Le (5 stars, photo-only, Jul 17). BREWHOUSE COFFEE: 1 unreplied -- Christie Hahn (5 stars, Jul 19, 'A Perfect 10/10 Experience'). Coffee had ZERO unreplied yesterday, so this is genuinely new. Joe (5 stars, Jul 17) and Matthew (Jul 16) were already answered. Draft replies for all 3 are in the Mon Jul 20 brief. NOTHING POSTED -- Joal approves every reply.

unanswered.author: Ronald Johnson
unanswered.snippet: Went for the world cup final got there a little late but the place had tables with no direct view of the game. Secondly just ordered burgers and a pitcher of beer, burgers were OK, beer was flat. Thankfully I was in with good company and none of that mattered

unanswered.snippet: (photo-only review -- 5 stars, no written text, 4 photos attached)

unanswered.snippet: A Perfect 10/10 Experience! If you are looking for a spot that takes both its craft and its... (truncated in notification)
